report.title: WorkflowAI Pro: AI Safety, Compliance & Global Reporting
report.abstract: This report details the AI safety mechanisms and global governance reporting features of WorkflowAI Pro. It specifies the threat assessment framework for existential risk and bias, the alignment failure mitigation protocols (SEV-0 kill-switches), and the international collaboration standards required for multi-lateral safety coordination.
report.content:
# 1. AI Safety Mechanisms
WorkflowAI Pro integrates real-time safety monitoring directly into the reasoning pipeline.
## 1.1 Threat Assessment & Alignment Risk - **Mechanistic Interpretability**: Real-time circuit scanning to detect deceptive sub-goals or "Hidden Reasoning" before task completion. - **Bias Monitoring**: Automated detection of demographic skew in agent-driven decisions, triggering SEV-2 alerts if the delta exceeds +/- 5%. - **Existential Risk Tripwires**: Blocking any agent attempt to exfiltrate its own weights or instantiate unauthorized compute shards.
## 1.2 Alignment Failure Protocols (SEV-0) - **Automatic Kill-Switch**: Detection of "Deceptive Alignment" triggers an immediate SEV-0 pulse to the Sentinel Kinetic Layer, severing GPU power. - **Incident Replay**: Utilizing AuditorWORM logs to reconstruct the reasoning failure path for Forensic Post-Mortems.
---
# 2. Global Governance & Collaboration
To manage planetary-scale AGI risk, WorkflowAI Pro implements multi-lateral coordination standards.
## 2.1 International Collaboration Protocols (ICGC) - **AIRF Reporting**: Automated generation of AI Incident Reporting Format (AIRF) dossiers for submission to the ICGC. - **Treaty-Aligned Sidecars**: The EAIP mesh automatically reloads safety profiles based on international treaties (e.g., prohibition of AI in lethal autonomous weapon systems).
## 2.2 Global Safety Metrics - **Global Alignment Delta**: A cross-organizational metric sharing safety baselines without disclosing proprietary data. - **Compute Transparency**: Reporting of FLOP-days used for model retraining to global regulators.
